title: U.S.-Cuba Relations
summary: 1959 – 2026 Since Fidel Castro’s ascent to power in 1959, U.S.-Cuba ties have endured a nuclear crisis, a long U.S. economic embargo, and persistent political hostilities. The diplomatic relationship thawed under President Barack Obama, but many restrictions have since been renewed.

title: Birthright Citizenship in the United States: What to Know
summary: The Supreme Court rejected President Donald Trump’s efforts to end birthright citizenship as part of a broader crackdown on immigration. The ruling upholds a nearly 160-year legal precedent that guarantees U.S. citizenship to almost anyone born on U.S. soil.

title: In Ankara, Europe Faces an Accelerating U.S. Decoupling From NATO
